step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{A} Based on the evidence, peer review plays a central role in evaluating provider performance through standardized, data-driven metrics—including patient outcomes, diagnostic accuracy, procedural volumes, compliance with protocols, and incident reporting—and supports continuous quality improvement via structured feedback loops, root cause analyses, and targeted intervention plans. The Joint Commission mandates clear, fair, and transparent peer review processes as integral parts of credentialing and privilege oversight, emphasizing its utility in enhancing patient safety, professionalism, and clinical effectiveness.
